source_name: Code4rena Noya Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
finding_title: NoyaValueOracle.getValue returns an incorrect price when a multi-token route is used
summary: The NoyaValueOracle.getValue is used to convert the price of an asset to baseToken, using token routing when necessary.
root_cause: incorrect accounting or validation logic in NoyaValueOracle.getValue returns an incorrect price when a multi-token route is used, causing the protocol to compute or enforce the wrong state transition
broken_invariants: the oracle path must not allow the condition described by "NoyaValueOracle.getValue returns an incorrect price when a multi-token route is used" to change user balances, collateralization, or protocol availability unexpectedly
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
state changes must remain internally consistent across all affected accounting buckets and external integrations
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: The NoyaValueOracle.getValue is used to convert the price of an asset to baseToken, using token routing when necessary.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: Replace asset with quotingToken: function _getValue(address asset, address baseToken, uint256 amount, address[] memory sources) internal view returns (uint256 value) { uint256 initialValue = amount; address quotingToken = asset; for (uint256 i = 0; i < sources.length; i++) { - initialValue = _getValue(asset, sources[i], initialValue); + initialValue = _getValue(quotingToken, sources[i], initialValue); quotingToken = sources[i]; } return _getValue(quotingToken, baseToken, initialValue); }
